commit.message:
fix: transactions tagging toolbar — review feedback (import type, asChild, aria-label, doc sync) (#177)

* feat: transactions table performance, row selection, and component extraction

- Reduce default page size from 5000 to 100 with user-controllable selector (25/50/100/250/Show All)
- Memoize filteredData, sortedData, paginatedData, and totals with useMemo
- Make pagination controls and thead sticky for better UX
- Replace Table wrapper with raw table for proper sticky behavior
- Implement row selection: click to select, Shift+click for range, Ctrl/Cmd+click for multi-select
- Fix CSS specificity bug where tbody hover overrode selection highlights
- Selection-aware tagging toolbar: operates on selected rows when present, falls back to all filtered rows

Extracted components/hooks:
- PaginationControls.tsx: Pagination UI with page size dropdown
- useRowSelection.ts: Selection hook with click/shift/ctrl logic
- useColumnVisibility.ts: 16 column-empty checks as one memoized hook
- TransactionsSummaryCards.tsx: 4-card summary grid (Net/Credits/Debits/Rows)
- TransactionsTaggingToolbar.tsx: Selection-aware tagging toolbar with confirm dialog
- useTransactionFilters.ts: 14 filter states + filteredData useMemo
- DeleteTransactionDialog.tsx: Delete confirmation AlertDialog
- transactionExport.ts: Pure CSV/JSON export utility functions
- TransactionsPageToolbar.tsx: Toolbar with year/tag/filter selectors and action buttons

* docs: update TransactionsTable.md for new pagination, row selection, and extracted components

* docs: add hook references to Filtering section

* docs: update FinanceTool.md and Tags.md for row selection and selection-aware tagging

* docs: promote Row Selection to its own ## section in FinanceTool.md

* fix: applyTag test combobox selector and improve tagging toolbar labels

* Fix pre-existing bug: restore consumed '::' token when not followed by identifier

In finalizeInstantiatedAliasType, when peek() == '::', the token was
consumed via advance() but if the next token was not an identifier the
code fell through silently, dropping the '::'. Use save/restore token
position to roll back when the speculative parse fails.

* Simplify reference check to single enum comparison

---------

commit.message:
Add Dependabot auto-merge workflow (#318)

Dependabot PRs with all checks passing should be merged automatically
without manual intervention.

## Changes

- **New workflow** `.github/workflows/dependabot.yml`: triggers on
`pull_request`, skips non-Dependabot actors, and calls `gh pr merge
--auto --squash` to enable GitHub's native auto-merge — the merge fires
only after all required status checks pass

```yaml
jobs:
  dependabot:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.actor == 'dependabot[bot]'
    steps:
      - name: auto-merge
        run: gh pr merge --auto --squash "$PR_URL"
        env:
          PR_URL: ${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
```

Requires `contents: write` and `pull-requests: write` permissions. Style
follows existing workflows (`on:` list notation, same job structure).

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: hzhangxyz <11623447+hzhangxyz@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Enable testTagsAsResourceId for Maestro id: selectors (#308)

Adds testTagsAsResourceId = true to Scaffold's semantics modifier
to expose Compose testTags as resource IDs in the accessibility tree.
This allows Maestro flows to find elements using id: selectors
(e.g., id: 'nav_history', id: 'search_bar').

Without this, Maestro's id: selector cannot find testTag elements
because Compose does not expose testTags as resource-id by default.

Fixes #307

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add JSON file-based config (spectral.json / $SPECTRAL_CONFIG) as env-var fallback
- Embed dashboard web UI (static/index.html) served at /ui/
- Add spectral-desktop launcher binary that starts the server and opens the browser

- Extend /admin/tenants to support POST (create) and DELETE (delete) tenant
- Add store.DeleteTenant() and tenantManager.evict() for safe tenant removal
- Add 9 new spectralctl commands: chain-verify, blockchain-export, routes-export,
  tenant-list, tenant-create, tenant-delete, chain-search, agent-status
- Add blockchain.VerifyChain() and SearchTransactions()
- Add event history ring-buffer (Broker.History, NewBrokerWithHistory)
- Add mesh per-peer health tracking (PeerStat, Node.PeerHealth)
- Add agent.Registry.CountByTenant()
- Add .gitignore (data/, *.db, __pycache__, binaries)
- Expand integration tests: event history, batch route delete, agent get-by-id,
  agent status endpoint, blockchain verify, blockchain search

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: autostop triggers from morning radio automation instead of media_title (#29)

The media_title condition didn't match (likely 'Evropa 2 - Radio' vs
'Evropa 2'), so the autostop never fired.

Simplified approach: trigger when the morning radio automation's
last_triggered attribute changes, then delay 2 hours and stop both
players. No conditions needed since the parent automation already
handles time/weekday checks.

Mode changed to 'restart' so re-triggering the radio resets the
2-hour timer.

Refs: #25

Co-authored-by: Petr Vavro <petrvavro@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
fix(autoupdate): set UpdateStatus based on NupkgPath, add package filter for workflow_dispatch

Previously, UpdateStatus was set to 'Update Available' when AU detected a newer remote version,
even if no .nupkg was built (e.g. version already exists in Chocolatey community feed).
This caused 'dotnet nuget push' to fail with a null path error.

Fix: derive UpdateStatus from NupkgPath so push steps only run when a package was actually built.

Also adds a package input to workflow_dispatch so individual packages can be tested manually.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

v3.1.0: Fix scrapling tiers, add SEARCH, cache, text extraction upgrades

- Fix scrapling dynamic tier: use DynamicFetcher().async_fetch() (was broken)
- Fix scrapling stealth tier: use StealthyFetcher().async_fetch() (was broken)
- Convert timeout from seconds to milliseconds for browser-based fetchers
- Add SEARCH capability to ScraplingEngine via DuckDuckGo
- Add DuckDuckGo fallback to bb-browser search (upstream adapters broken)
- Upgrade text extraction: trafilatura first, regex fallback
- Add SQLite-based fetch cache (app/cache.py)
- Integrate cache into EngineManager.fetch_with_fallback
- Fix SSL certs for curl_cffi on Windows (auto-configure CURL_CA_BUNDLE)
- Add configurable rate limiting via env vars
- Fix MCP health endpoint version to 3.1.0, add cache stats
- Fix test: handle engines list format in MCP health check
- Fix MultiSourceDiscovery: use available SEARCH engines when named engine not found
- Test suite: 93.5% pass rate (29/31), up from 90.3%

commit.message:
캐릭터 콤보박스: 매칭 우선 정렬 + 전체 목록 표시, 버전 0.4.1 (#11)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: YJSoft <5893514+YJSoft@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(build): prevent 0-byte static pages from I/O contention

- Reduce parallel write batch from 300 to 200 to lower I/O pressure
- Add sync repair pass after flush: detects and rewrites 0-byte files
- Log individual write failures with file paths (was only logging count)
- flatContentPlugin: skip copying 0-byte source files to flat paths

Root cause: writeFile uses O_TRUNC which creates 0-byte files before
writing content. Under heavy I/O contention (153K+ parallel writes),
some writes fail after truncation, leaving 0-byte files that Google
indexes as soft-404s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Copilot/analyze encode decode pipeline (#277)

* Fix COD marker (MCT, progression, layers), QCD epsilon values, packet header format with per-band ordering, and add componentIndex/resolutionLevel to J2KCodeBlock

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Raster-Lab/J2KSwift/sessions/100113ed-8c21-4adc-bdc2-7bf9300ee75e

Co-authored-by: SureshKViswanathan <257696045+SureshKViswanathan@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add J2KTagTree, CodecService, and fix encoder pipeline codestream generation

- Add J2KTagTree for tag tree encoding used in packet headers
- Add CodecService for test app codec integration
- Fix encoder pipeline: correct tile-part lengths, SOP/EPH markers,
  packet header tag tree encoding, and bitstream assembly
- Add J2KTestAppModels extensions for codec result tracking
- Add encoder pipeline tests for codestream validation
- Update Package.swift for new source targets

* Fix OpenJPEG interoperability and add benchmark comparison

* Improve reversible rate control and decoder robustness

* Fix 6 encoder/decoder root causes and add clinical validation suite

Encoder fixes:
- Use irreversible 9/7 wavelet as default (was reversible 5/3)
- Fix coarse quantization step sizes for proper quality
- Use actual image distortion in PCRD rate control (was model-based)
- Fix lossless didSet observer clearing reversible filter setting
- Use actual component bit depth in EBCOT (was hardcoded to 8)

Decoder fixes:
- Align to byte boundary after empty packet flag

CLI fixes:
- PGM/PPM bit depth detection via ceil(log2(maxVal+1))

Validation (all passing):
- 95/95 unit tests pass
- 208/208 cross-decoder + stress + hi-res validation tests pass
- 224/224 clinical validation tests pass (5 modalities, pathology variants)
- Medical-grade certification: CT ✅, X-ray ✅, MRI ⚠, US ⚠, PET ⚠
- Lossless mode: perfect reconstruction across all modalities

New scripts:
- Scripts/medical_benchmark.py — CT/MRI/US benchmark vs OpenJPEG
- Scripts/validation_suite.py — cross-decoder, stress, hi-res tests
- Scripts/clinical_validation.py — 5-modality clinical certification suite

New metrics: PSNR, SSIM, MAE, CNR, Edge Preservation Index, Histogram Correlation
Reports: CLINICAL_VALIDATION_REPORT.md, VALIDATION_REPORT.md, MEDICAL_BENCHMARK.md

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: SureshKViswanathan <257696045+SureshKViswanathan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: raster <raster@rasters-MacBook-Air.local>

commit.message:
feat(salary): replace estimates with USTAT 2024 official Ticino data

Replace rough salary estimates with official data from USTAT Ticino
(Rilevazione svizzera della struttura dei salari, cubi_RSS_02, 2024).

Key changes:
- TICINO_SECTOR_MEDIANS: all 16 sectors updated with USTAT p50 data
  by NOGA 2008 sector × position level (junior/mid/senior)
- Ticino discount corrected: was ×0.90 (10% below national), actual
  is ~0.82 (18% below) per USTAT 2024 (CHF 5,393 vs ~6,600 national)
- Min/max range widened: ×0.80/×1.25 (was ×0.85/×1.15) based on
  actual p25/p75 interquartile ratios from USTAT data
- salaryData.ts: all 15 sectors × 4 professions recalibrated to
  match USTAT Ticino figures (generally lower than previous estimates)
- Added NOGA code references per sector for traceability
- Source attribution: USTAT cubi_RSS_02 2024, BFS LSE, Salarium

Notable corrections (sectors most affected):
- Education: was overstated by 20-36% (Ticino education pays less)
- Engineering: junior/mid were 10-18% too high
- Hospitality: senior was overstated by ~29%
- Finance senior: was understated by ~44% (USTAT shows CHF 184k)
- Retail: was understated by 18-37% across all levels

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
